title: santolina rosmarinifolia subsp. castellana
intro: Santolina rosmarinifolia subsp. castellana is a subspecies of shrubby perennial plant in the sunflower family, known for its silvery-gray foliage and yellow flowers. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. Its medicinal properties have been associated with various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting digestive health. Santolina rosmarinifolia subsp. castellana can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in aromatherapy to relieve stress and promote relaxation.
